## Goal
Provide a single source of truth for backlog structure across epics, detailed issues, and sprint gates to deliver the Super IDE platform safely and fast.

## Program Outcomes
- Enterprise-grade thin-client workstation with portal control plane.
- Regulated-ready SaaS and PaaS operating model.
- Cost-efficient traffic architecture with measurable improvements.
- Reliable self-healing with bounded automation and auditability.

## Epic Stack
- [ ] #015 Control plane contract and policy authority.
- [ ] #016 Managed workstation runtime and Open in App lifecycle.
- [ ] #017 Security and compliance baseline for regulated operation.
- [ ] #018 SLOG, remediation, and reliability engineering.
- [ ] #019 FinOps and traffic optimization architecture.

## Sprint Gates
- [ ] #020 Sprint gate: foundation contract and threat model.
- [ ] #021 Sprint gate: runtime and bootstrap MVP.
- [ ] #022 Sprint gate: reliability and hardening.
- [ ] #023 Sprint gate: regulated SaaS and PaaS readiness.

## Exit Criteria
- [ ] All epic acceptance criteria met.
- [ ] All sprint gates passed with evidence links.
- [ ] Architecture, security, and operations sign-off complete.
- [ ] Customer-facing and internal runbooks complete.

## Risks
- Scope expansion without contract discipline.
- Security controls bypassed by convenience paths.
- Cost optimization done without measurement proof.

## Closure
Program closes only when all dependent epics and sprint gates close with testable artifacts and operational evidence.
